chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: Next we are going to cover the concept of Axis in multi-dimensional arrays.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: This is a very important concept and we will use it frequently in the future.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: Basically, before covering the axis, let's talk about the practical need of multi-dimensional arrays.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: We have already seen a use case that whenever we have to create images,

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: our computers don't understand images,

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: so we convert images into a matrix of numbers and feed it to our system or algorithm.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: Only then the machine learning algorithm can work on it.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: So whenever we have to feed an image,

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: if we have a black and white image,

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: we can feed it to our system in the form of a 2D array.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: We can feed our machine learning algorithm,

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: so we will need a 2D array.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: For example, we have taken the example of these two pictures.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: This is a black and white image and this is a colored image.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: If we want to feed this black and white image,

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: we can feed it as a single matrix,

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: in which every pixel has a particular value from 0 to 255,

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: which will go to our system.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: For example, these black values will be represented by 0,

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: and the rest of the pixels, because they all have a white value,

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: they are all white, so their value will be 1.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: And instead of 7, the 7 values will be represented in the form of 0 in our matrix.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: But when we have to feed colored pictures to our system,

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: to our machine learning algorithms,

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: we feed them in the form of RGB values.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: RGB means red, green, blue.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: We can feed any colored image to our system in the form of three color combinations.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: Now, RGB means that we send a set of three values for each particular pixel.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: If we talk about this pixel, it is a blue pixel,

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: its values will be made up of three different values.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: The value of this blue pixel is mostly blue,

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: so its value will be 0, 0, 255.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: In RGB, the red is 0, the green is 0, but the blue is full,

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: because this pixel is a complete blue pixel.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: So, this is how the value of the blue pixel will go.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: If we talk about the yellow pixel,

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: then what will be the value of the yellow pixel?

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: Yellow is made up of red and green.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: RGB of yellow is actually 255, 255, and 0.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: So, all these pixels will have the RGB value of yellow,

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: and all the other pixels will have the RGB value of blue.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: Now, when we want to send RGB values,

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: we cannot send them in a single matrix.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: This means that we will divide this whole image into a single matrix,

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: just for red color,

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: we will divide it into a single matrix, just for green color,

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: and we will divide it into a single matrix, just for blue color.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: We will understand this whole concept in full detail later.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: So, if it is a little difficult for us to understand for the first time,

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: then it's not a problem at all.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: We will cover this in detail later.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: Now, we are discussing a small example.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: So, whenever we want to send colored images,

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: basically, we send three different sets of matrix.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: One is a proper matrix of red color,

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: one is a proper matrix of green color,

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: and one is a proper matrix of blue color.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: So, we basically send a combination of 0, 1, 2, 3 matrices.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: So, whenever we want to feed a colored image,

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: we feed it in the form of 3D array to our system.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: And that is why we need 3D arrays.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: This is why we need 2D arrays.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: Let's suppose we want to create a simple machine learning algorithm

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: that plays chess.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: The other player in the chess game beats the other player.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: Now, how will we represent chess in computers?

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: How will we represent chess in Python code?

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: We are basically going to represent it as a grid,

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: as an 8x8 grid.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: We will represent chess in this way.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: Only then we will be able to train our machine learning model.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: In fact, we will be able to write code for chess in Python

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: when we have this grid.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: And how will we make this grid?

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: We will make it in the form of a 2D array.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: So, there are many use cases of single dimensional arrays, 2D arrays,

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: in fact, 3D arrays as well.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: Because 3D arrays are also very important.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: And that is why we talk about multi-dimensional arrays in NumPy.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: It is not that we are increasing the complexity in the air

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: that all the work was being done with 1D arrays

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: and we are just discussing 2D and 3D arrays for fun.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: No.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: The importance of 2D and 3D arrays is very high

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: whenever we talk about data

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: which is used to train machine learning and deep learning models.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: So, that is why n-dimensional arrays are very important

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: and that is why we are discussing them.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: So, the concept of axis in n-dimensional arrays

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: is very similar to the dimension concept.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: When we are talking about axis in our n-dimensional array,

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: we can say that we are actually talking about the dimensions of that particular array.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: Now, we must have heard the word axis.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: The plural of that is this axis with e.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: What does axis mean?

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: We think of axis as a dimension or a direction.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: In 1D arrays, we say that there is only one axis which is axis 0.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: This is axis 0.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: That means all the numbers exist on a single axis.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: In 2D arrays, we say that there are two axes.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: One is axis 0 and the other is axis 1.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: Axis 0 means that we are talking about rows

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: and axis 1 means that we are talking about columns.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: And all the operations can be performed axis wise.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: We will see an example of that.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: All our 3D arrays, we say that they have three axes.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: One is axis 0, one is axis 1 and the third is axis 2.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: Axis 0 basically represents the depth or the number of layers.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: Axis 1 is the row in each layer or in each depth.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: We represent the row in each layer with axis 1.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: And we represent the column in each layer with axis 2.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: For example, we have a matrix.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: We have some rows in this matrix.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: We have rows like this and columns like this.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: There are some operations which we want to perform column wise.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: For example, there is an operation which we want to perform like this.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: We will perform this operation on axis 0.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: Axis 0 performs the operation vertically.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: And axis 1 performs the operation horizontally.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: This can be a little confusing as a beginner.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: Because when we talk about rows, we generally imagine them horizontally.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: But here, performing the operation vertically means that

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: we are performing the operation on the 0th index of each row.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: We are performing the operation on the first index of each row.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: And similarly, we perform the column wise operation like this.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: We are performing the operations horizontally.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: This is the meaning of axis in 2D array.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: When we talk about 3D arrays,

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: we can basically imagine a 3D array as a combination of 2D arrays.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: For example, if this is the 2D array of my 0th index,

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: this is the 2D array of my first index.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: Similarly, we can create another 2D array.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: This is the 2D array of my second index.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: So, these multiple 2D arrays together form a 3D array.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: For example, if we take an image as an example.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: Let's suppose these are the arrays for our RGB values.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: How will these RGB values be stored?

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: They will be stored in a 3D array.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: Now, 3D array is not a fancy thing.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: It is just a combination of multiple 2D arrays that forms a 3D array.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: And how will we access the values in a 3D array?

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: Let's suppose we have the 3D array.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: So, in the 3D array, first of all, we select our axis 0.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: Which is, which layer are we talking about?

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: We are talking about the R layer, G layer, B layer.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: Let's suppose we are talking about this layer, this 2D array.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: So, here we will select 1.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: After that, we will define our second value, which will be the row in this layer.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: We have defined it, we are talking about this 2D array.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: Let's suppose we want to access this cell.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: Now, this cell is row 2.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: So, here the row value will come.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: And what is its column?

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: Its column is 0.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: So, here our column value will come.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: And in this way, we access the values in the 3D array.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: So, this is basically how axis works in multidimensional arrays.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: Let's see this whole concept in the form of code.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: We are going to look into multidimensional arrays.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: Now, in our multidimensional arrays, we create a new array.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: Let's call it array 2D.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: So, in array 2D, we will normally create some elements.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: Let's make an array of 3x3 size.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: And we have 7, 8, 9.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: So, we can print the 2D array.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: So, this is the 2D array that we have.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: Now, in our numpy, we have a very simple operation, which is the sum operation.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: If we want, we can print sum np.sum of the 2D array.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: So, sum will be calculated for all the numbers, which is equal to 45.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: Now, we can perform this sum operation column and row wise with the help of axis.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: How?

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: Let's suppose we want to calculate sum of all the columns.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: So, how will we calculate the sum of columns?

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: If we assume that this is just a 2D array, then we have to calculate the sum of every column.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: The sum of columns is basically the sum of the first element of every row.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: Then the sum of the second element of every row.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: So, basically we want to calculate the sum vertically, which means we need to use axis 0.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: How will we use axis 0?

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: We can write np.sum, in which we will pass our first parameter, 2D array.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: And along with it, we can pass our axis value, which will be 0.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: So, we will get sum of columns, which we can print if we want.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: So, we had the whole array and this is the sum of columns.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: That means if we sum 1, 4 and 7, this will be 12.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: If we sum 2, 5 and 8, it will be 15.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: If we sum 3, 6 and 9, it will be 18.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: Similarly, we did the sum of columns.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: We can do the sum of rows in the same way.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: We can calculate sum of rows, in which axis will be 1.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: So, in this way, we will get the sum of rows.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: Sum of rows means the sum of 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, which is 15.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: And the sum of 7, 8, 9, which is equal to 24.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: So, this is how we can perform operations axis wise.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: We can also do slicing axis wise.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: For example, to slice this 2D array, let's suppose we need a particular part of this 2D array.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: That means we need some particular rows and some particular columns.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: Now, the 2D array that we have is 3 x 3.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: Let's take the same example.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: We have a 2D array, which is 3 x 3.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: Something like this.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: And in this 3 x 3 array, let's suppose we want to access these two elements.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: So, for these two elements, we know which rows we need to access.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: We need to access 0 and 1 rows.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: So, first of all, we define the rows in our array.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: Where will the rows come from?

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: In 0, 1, and 2.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: We want to access the rows from 0 to 1.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: So, we will have 0, 1.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: And comma.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: Which columns do we want to access?

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: We want to access the first column.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: So, that is 1, 2, 2.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: And in this way, we can print these elements.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: So, we have 0, 2, 1, 2.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: If we enter, we will get 2 and 5.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: Our numbers are basically 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: We talked about how we want to access these two numbers.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: So, we will get these two numbers in this way in this format.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: So, in this way, we use our access values.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: In multidimensional arrays, we saw all this in the case of 2D array.

chunks.title: Multi-dimensional
chunks.text: We can perform the same operations on 3D array.

Basically, before covering the axis, let's talk about the practical need of multi-dimensional arrays. We have already seen a use case that whenever we have to create images, our computers don't understand images, so we convert images into a matrix of numbers and feed it to our system or algorithm. Only then the machine learning algorithm can work on it. So whenever we have to feed an image, if we have a black and white image, we can feed it to our system in the form of a 2D array. We can feed our machine learning algorithm, so we will need a 2D array. For example, we have taken the example of these two pictures. This is a black and white image and this is a colored image. If we want to feed this black and white image, we can feed it as a single matrix, in which every pixel has a particular value from 0 to 255, which will go to our system. For example, these black values will be represented by 0, and the rest of the pixels, because they all have a white value, they are all white, so their value will be 1. And instead of 7, the 7 values will be represented in the form of 0 in our matrix. But when we have to feed colored pictures to our system, to our machine learning algorithms, we feed them in the form of RGB values. RGB means red, green, blue. We can feed any colored image to our system in the form of three color combinations. Now, RGB means that we send a set of three values for each particular pixel. If we talk about this pixel, it is a blue pixel, its values will be made up of three different values. The value of this blue pixel is mostly blue, so its value will be 0, 0, 255. If we talk about the yellow pixel, then what will be the value of the yellow pixel? Yellow is made up of red and green. RGB of yellow is actually 255, 255, and 0. That means all the numbers exist on a single axis. In 2D arrays, we say that there are two axes. One is axis 0 and the other is axis 1. Axis 0 means that we are talking about rows and axis 1 means that we are talking about columns. And all the operations can be performed axis wise. We will see an example of that. And axis 1 performs the operation horizontally. This can be a little confusing as a beginner. Because when we talk about rows, we generally imagine them horizontally. But here, performing the operation vertically means that we are performing the operation on the 0th index of each row. We are performing the operation on the first index of each row. So, in the 3D array, first of all, we select our axis 0. Which is, which layer are we talking about? We are talking about the R layer, G layer, B layer.
